questions.question: What is the primary difference between an 'opening' morphological operation and a 'closing' operation in morphological image processing?

A binary image is represented as a class of pixels where pixels have a value of either 0 (background) or 255 (foreground). What is the purpose of using 'hole filling' in morphological image processing?
What is the effect of applying a morphological dilation operation to an image containing only foreground pixels?
